channel.title: code on mahmood
channel.description: Recent content in code on mahmood

item.title: data structures
item.description:
\(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{an}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{ng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(
ewcommand\dif[1]{\:	extrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{ceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter loor{\lfloor}{floor}\) \(
ewcommand{\ihat}{\hat{	extbf{i}}}\) \(
ewcommand{\jhat}{\hat{	extbf{j}}}\) \(
ewcommand{\khat}{\hat{	extbf{k}}}\) \(
ewcommand{hat}{\hat{	extbf{r}}}\) \(
ewcommand{	hetahat}{ oldsymbol{\hat{	heta}}}\) for most data structures i havent implemented deletion yet.. also its good to note that most of the code isnt optimized to hell, but it doesnt really matter since its only for education purposes and you should probably be using the data structures provided by the programming language’s standard library 1 properties of for every 2 numbers \(m,n\) and every number \(x\) \[ \ceil*{ rac{\ceil*{ rac{x}{m}}}{n}} = \ceil*{ rac{x}{m \cdot n}} \qquad  loor*{ rac{ loor*{ rac{x}{m}}}{n}} =  loor*{ rac{x}{m \cdot n}} \] \[ \ceil*{ rac{\ceil*{ rac{x}{2^i}}}{2}} = \ceil*{ rac{x}{2^{i+1}}} \qquad  loor*{ rac{ loor*{ rac{x}{2^i}}}{2}} =  loor*{ rac{x}{2^{i+1}}} \] the following is true for every normal number \(n\) and any number \(x\)  egin{gather}  loor*{x}+n =  loor*{x+n} \qquad \ceil*{x}+n=\ceil*{x+n}\ n- loor*{x} = \ceil*{n-x} \qquad n-\ceil*{x} =  loor*{n-x}\  loor*{x} \geq \ceil*{x}-1 \qquad \ceil*{x} \leq  loor*{x}+1\  loor*{x} > x-1 \qquad \ceil*{x} < x+1\  loor*{x} \leq x \qquad \ceil*{x} \geq x\  loor*{x} = n \iff n \leq x < n+1 \qquad \ceil*{x} = n \iff n-1 < x \leq n \end{gather}

item.title: tikz
item.description:
\(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{an}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{ng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(
ewcommand\dif[1]{\:	extrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{ceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter loor{\lfloor}{floor}\) \(
ewcommand{\ihat}{\hat{	extbf{i}}}\) \(
ewcommand{\jhat}{\hat{	extbf{j}}}\) \(
ewcommand{\khat}{\hat{	extbf{k}}}\) \(
ewcommand{hat}{\hat{	extbf{r}}}\) \(
ewcommand{	hetahat}{ oldsymbol{\hat{	heta}}}\) for reference: refcard tikz is used to draw graphics in latex documents it can be imported like this: \usepackage{tikz} 1 primitive shapes drawing primitive shapes like lines/rectangles is very simple 1.1 lines here |- generates a broken line while -- generates a straight line 	ikz\draw (0,0) -- (3,2) -- (5,0) |- (4,3); 	ikz\draw[->] (-2,0) -- (-3,0); 1.
